Did You Know?: Hazel trains in a garage with her teammates under the guidance of their three coaches Anh, Huy and Long Nguyen. She joined in 2005 and has performed Taekwondo demonstration at more than 150 national and international events including the U.S. Open, U.S. Nationals, International Taekwondo Festival, World Taekwondo Hanmadang, and Suwon Championships in Korea. Also, she has trained at the World Taekwondo Headquarters (Kukkiwon) and Keimyung University while she was in Korea in 2009.

At this point I’d try to shake the hand of whoever it is that’s reading this, but unfortunately I cannot because I’m eating something. And this is only a message but why limit yourself? I’m a freshman on the men’s Nordic Ski Team here at SLU and some of the things I like to do outside of skiing and training include running, hiking, running what I should really be hiking, and socializing with the interesting saints of 2017. If you ever decide to make me food, I personally really enjoy a good chicken Peruvian, or just pineapple. Either one is fine if it’s the chicken Peruvian.

Biographical Information

Liam Cunningham grew up in a hockey family and began playing sled hockey at age seven, after being diagnosed with a degenerative bone disorder in his legs. He joined the Minnesota Wild sled ice hockey program and seven years later made the U.S. Men's Development Sled Hockey Team.

Cunningham made his first U.S. National Sled Hockey Team in 2023 at age 15, making his international debut at the International Para Hockey Cup. At 17, he is the youngest player named to the U.S. sled hockey team for the 2025 World Para Ice Hockey Championships.

Career Highlights
  • Two-time Para Hockey Cup champion (2023, 2024)
  • Two-time IPH Cup champion (2023, 2024)

Quick Facts
  • Position: Forward
  • Daughter of Bill and Gretchen Curl
  • Has two brothers, Byrne and Cullen, and one sister, Brenna
  • Won three national championships at the University of Wisconsin
Biographical Information

Britta Curl-Salemme grew up in Bismarck, North Dakota. Every winter, Curl-Salemme’s father, Bill, would set up a rink in their backyard. Following her older brother, Bryne, she decided to become a hockey player, playing on boys’ youth hockey teams until the eighth grade. 

Curl-Salemme played five seasons at the University of Wisconsin, where she won three national championships with the Badgers. She left college as a two-time captain, and her college coach, Mark Johnson, one of the heroes for Team USA 1980 "Miracle on Ice" squad, described Curl-Salemmme as one of the best captains in Wisconsin history:  “She’s genuine, she’s real, she’s considerate, she thinks of other people before herself.”

Curl-Salemme made her IIHF World Championship debut at the 2021 IIHF World Championships for the U.S. women’s hockey team. She was invited as one of 28 players to join the Olympic residency program before the Olympic Winter Games Beijing 2022 and was selected for her first Olympic team for the Olympic Winter Games Milano Cortina 2026.

Professionally, Curl-Salemme plays in the PWHL for the Minnesota Frost, where she won the Walter Cup and was named to the PWHL All-Rookie Team in her first season in the league. 

In September 2024, she married former Wisconsin wrestler Andrew Salemme. 

World Championships Experience

  • Years of participation: Team - Women 2021, 2023, 2024, 2025
  • Medals: 4 (2 gold, 2 silver)
    • Gold – 2025 (Team - Women); 2023 (Team - Women)
    • Silver – 2024 (Team - Women); 2021 (Team - Women)
Olympic Experience
  • 1-time Olympian; 1-time Olympic medalist (1 gold)
    • Olympic Games Milan 2026, gold (Team - Women)

Biographical Information

Steph Curry is widely regarded as one of the greatest shooters in basketball history. Known for revolutionizing the game with his three-point shooting, Curry has set numerous NBA records and earned multiple MVP awards. His leadership and sharpshooting have led the Golden State Warriors to multiple NBA championships, solidifying his legacy as a generational talent.

Stephen Curry is the son of former NBA player Dell Curry and has a younger brother, Seth Curry, who also plays in the NBA. Off the court, Stephen is known for his strong Christian faith, philanthropy, and involvement in various charitable initiatives. Curry is also an entrepreneur with ventures in media, technology, and the culinary world.

bio.fun_fact: Serves in the U.S. Air Force World Class Athlete Program
bio.biography:
Quick Facts

  • Specialty: Skeleton

  • Daughter of John and Debbie Curtis

  • She has four siblings

  • Competed in track and field at Springfield College before she was introduced to bobsledding by the strength and conditioning coach who suggested she try the combine

  • Became the first Black athlete to represent Team USA in skeleton at the Olympic Winter Games Beijing 2022

  • Serves in the U.S. Air Force World Class Athlete Program

Biographical Information

Kelly Curtis is an American skeleton athlete and Olympian. Born in Princeton, New Jersey, she grew up in an athletic family and excelled in track and field. Curtis competed at Springfield College as a heptathlete (where she won the 2011 Penn Relays Heptathlon) before discovering the skeleton. 

She made history at the Olympic Winter Games Beijing 2022 as the first Black athlete to compete for Team USA in skeleton. She has also represented the U.S. in multiple world cup and international competitions, consistently ranking among the top competitors.

In addition to her athletic career, Curtis serves in the U.S. Air Force through the World Class Athlete Program, balancing her military service with elite-level training.

International Judo Federation Competition History: Nina Cutro-Kelly

Personal: In 2021, Cutro-Kelly qualified for her first Olympic Team at 36 years old, making her the oldest U.S. athlete ever to represent USA Judo at the Olympic Games. The nine-time World Team member continued her competitive success in 2022 when she reached the podium at the Pan American Championships, winning her fifth career medal at the event. Cutro-Kelly, who has 50% hearing loss in both ears, also made history in 2022 as the first U.S. judo athlete ever to win gold at the Deaflympics. 

In addition to training, Cutro-Kelly works as an athlete fellow in the legal department for LA28, the organizing committee for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympic and Paralympic Games.

  • Bronze - Senior Pan American Championships

Quick Facts
  • Has a dog named Fenway after the Stadium where the Boston Red Sox play
  • Is a HUGE Boston Celtics basketball fan and watches every single game
  • Her dad played professional baseball and was drafted by the Seattle Mariners in 1989
  • Started synchro because her neighbor who was older than her was doing it - nine years later they had a duet together and the neighbor now attends Ohio State
  • Loves to travel to new places and her family has a cabin in the mountains in BC
Olympic Experience
  • 1-time Olympian; 1-time Olympic medalist (1 silver)
    • Olympic Games Paris 2024, silver (Team - Open), 10th (Duet - Women)
World Championships Experience
  • 2022 FINA World Championships: 6th (Tech Team), 9th (Free Team), 5th (Highlight)

bio.education: Boonton High School (Boonton, N.J.) ‘97 Desales University ‘01, Criminal Justice
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Daughter of David and Barbara
  • Has two brothers, Christopher and Kevin
  • Married to Jake Czechowski, the U.S. Women’s Goalball Head Coach
  • Has a son, Jay
  • Born with nystagmus and diagnosed with cone/rod dystrophy in middle school
  • Learned about goalball while competing in track & field at the Sydney Games
  • Enjoys reading, listening to music, playing board games, shopping, playing sports and playing with her son
  • Grew up in a house on Banta Lane and her maiden name is Banta
  • Took part in the Olympic torch relay for the Olympic Winter Games Salt Lake City 2001 while in Princeton, New Jersey.
Paralympic Experience
  • 6-time Paralympian; 5-time Paralympic medalist (1 gold, 3 silver, 1 bronze)
    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, silver (Team - Women)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, bronze (Team - Women)
    • Paralympic Games London 2012, 5th (Team - Women)
    • Paralympic Games Beijing 2008, gold (Team - Women)
    • Paralympic Games Athens 2004, silver (Team - Women)
    • Paralympic Games Sydney 2000, silver (Discus Throw F12 - Women), 6th (Team - Women)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2018 – 6th (Team - Women)
  • Years of participation: Team - Women 2018
  • Top finish: 6th – 2018 (Team - Women)
More

USABA

Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Eric and Donna D'Agostino
  • Has two sisters, Lily and Julia
  • Seven-time NCAA champion and in 2012, became the first female distance runner from Dartmouth University to win an NCAA title
  • In 2013, became only the fifth woman in NCAA history to win back-to-back titles in the 5,000-meter
  • Was the seventh woman in NCAA history to win the indoor and outdoor 5,000 in the same academic year.
Olympic Experience
  • 1-time Olympian
    • Olympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 18th (5000m - Women)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2016 – 5th (3000m (Indoor) - Women)
  • Years of participation: 3000m (Indoor) - Women 2016; 5000m - Women 2015
  • Top finish: 5th – 2016 (3000m (Indoor) - Women)

Quick Facts
  • Started to play handball in 2017 at school in Brazil
  • After two years of playing, she attended tryouts for Brazilian handball club Pinheiros and made the team
  • Shortly afterwards, her family moved to the United States and she began to play for Boston Team Handball Club.
Career Highlights
  • World Junior Championship, 2022 (Team - Women)
